note: Test prompts for the skill. Each 'feature' field says which behaviour it exercises. After a change, run the prompt(s) whose feature your change touched, save the TSV output, then run validate.py on it. See README.md.

evals.name: code-escaping
evals.prompt:
Make me Anki flashcards from these C++ notes:

In C++, std::vector<T> is a dynamic array; to declare a vector of integers you write std::vector<int> v;. A map from string to int is std::map<std::string, int>. References use the & symbol: a function void swap(int& a, int& b) swaps two ints in place. The boolean expression a < b && b < c is true only when b lies strictly between a and c. The stream-extraction operator >> reads input, as in std::cin >> x;. Templates enable generic code: template<typename T> T maximum(T a, T b) returns the larger argument.
evals.expected_output: Basic TSV with literal <, >, & escaped to < > & so type names survive HTML rendering. Atomic Q&A cards. No em dashes. Hierarchical tags.

evals.name: vocab-reversed
evals.feature: reversed note type for vocabulary (Basic (and reversed card))
evals.prompt:
Turn this French vocab list into flashcards I can revise from:
la maison - house
manger - to eat
rapide - quick / fast
le chien - dog
apprendre - to learn
difficile - difficult
le livre - book
evals.expected_output: A reversed TSV using #notetype:Basic (and reversed card) so each pair becomes two cards (FR->EN and EN->FR). Atomic single-answer pairs.

evals.name: mixed-source
evals.feature: basic + cloze split, atomicity, cloze Back Extra, hierarchical tags, scope handling
evals.prompt: Can you make some study cards from this passage on the immune system?
evals.expected_output: Basic + cloze TSVs. Atomic cards for definitions/processes; cloze (HTML list, Back Extra populated) for genuine enumerations. Tags applied.

evals.name: overlapping-cloze
evals.feature: overlapping (sliding-window) cloze for a long ordered sequence; date stamping
evals.prompt: I need to memorise the order of the first ten elements of the periodic table for a chemistry exam - the exact sequence matters. Make me Anki cards.
evals.expected_output: Cloze TSV using sliding-window overlapping cloze (one c1 per note, hides one item, shows neighbours) rather than one giant stacked deletion. Hierarchical tags.
